title: TeraWulf (WULF) Stock: Q1 Revenue Rises as $3.1B Cash Position Supports Data Center Growth
text: TeraWulf reported Q1 2026 revenue of $34.01 million, marginally exceeding analyst estimates by 0.07%, while maintaining a strong cash position of $3.1 billion. The company's balance sheet showed $2.63 billion in cash and cash equivalents and $462.7 million in restricted cash. Despite posting a net loss of $427.7 million in Q1 2026 (which included a $216.3 million non-cash change in warrant fair value), the substantial cash position provides TeraWulf with substantial capital to fund its capital-intensive data center infrastructure development. Total liabilities reached $7.09 billion with a total debt load of $5.7 billion and quarterly interest expenses of $67.1 million. The company recorded $713 million in capital expenditures for the quarter while operating cash flow remained negative at $17.6 million. The strong cash position is critical for TeraWulf's transition strategy, enabling aggressive deployment of new HPC capacity across multiple sites.
